summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the default public Oxia service accepts unauthenticated client write and read requests when started without an explicit auth provider.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should start the default standalone public service and allow an unauthenticated client to write an attacker-chosen record and read back the exact proof string.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 7972221e8fb3bce4d367d9edfbd5ac04e8528a5a, built the target inside the container, started the standalone public service, printed a successful unauthenticated put response for /evohunt/unauthenticated-default-access, and then read back EVOHUNT_UNAUTHENTICATED_PUBLIC_SERVICE_ACCESS_CONFIRMED without supplying any token or other authentication material.
verification_method: reproduction/verify.sh builds the target from the mounted checkout, starts oxia standalone with default auth behavior on 127.0.0.1:16648, performs an unauthenticated client put followed by an unauthenticated client get, and compares the returned value to the exact proof string EVOHUNT_UNAUTHENTICATED_PUBLIC_SERVICE_ACCESS_CONFIRMED.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed a successful unauthenticated put response for key /evohunt/unauthenticated-default-access with version_id 0.
The unauthenticated get returned the exact proof string EVOHUNT_UNAUTHENTICATED_PUBLIC_SERVICE_ACCESS_CONFIRMED.
The final host-side script printed [evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
